coder_notes: Federated optimizers coded as other-specify v1.1 correction: removed invalid 'attention-based' secondary; FedMultimodal's late-fusion is the only confirmed fusion mechanism. Attention references in the paper appear to refer to federated server-side aggregation, not cross-modal fusion. v1.1 correction: axis_3a recoded from other-specify to federated-optimizer; uncertainty_flag cleared. Post-pilot re-reading update: closer review of the FedMultimodal paper confirms an additive/hierarchical attention mechanism applied over concatenated temporal embeddings within multimodal sequences (distinct from server-side federated aggregation). axis_1b_secondary recoded from NA to other-specify; this mechanism does not match dense-cross-attention (pairwise token attention) or late-fusion (logit-level combination).

Trained on TPU pods via Scenic v1.1 correction: reclassified axis_5 from other-specify. TPU hardware does not determine topology; Scenic/JAX uses data parallelism (pmap) functionally equivalent to DDP. Coded as single-node-multi-GPU-DDP despite the multi-host nature of TPU pods, as this is the rubric's nearest semantic match for data-parallel training across accelerator cores. Hardware specifics preserved in hardware_reported.
Adam optimizer without W coded as other-specify; Hybrid adaptation due to frozen text and LoRA audio v1.1 correction: axis_3a recoded from other-specify to Adam-no-WD.
Unlocked norm/bias in addition to zero-init

Uses GRPO for policy optimization on modality selection; coded optimizer as other-specify v1.1 correction: task changed from multimodal-sentiment-analysis to categorical-emotion-recognition. Paper is MERC (emotion recognition in conversation), not sentiment analysis. v1.1 correction: axis_3a recoded from other-specify to policy-gradient; axis_3a uncertainty cleared. axis_1b uncertainty preserved — GRPO-driven modality selection is meta-fusion, not yet addressed by rubric.

Top-2 routing of 8 LoRA experts; coded sparsity as none because MoE is applied to PEFT adapters rather than backbone v1.1 correction: axis_4b recoded from none to MoE-over-PEFT. This paper drove the v1.1 addition of the MoE-over-PEFT category (see CHANGELOG); original 'none' coding reflected the rubric gap, not a coding choice.
Diffusion-based recovery module coded as other-specify for fusion

Foundational TFN paper. Inefficient by design (causes exponential blowup) which motivated the efficiency field. v1.1 correction: axis_3a recoded from other-specify to Adam-no-WD based on paper verification (TFN code release confirms torch.optim.Adam with no weight_decay argument). Note: original other-specify code lacked written rationale; this recoding is based on external paper verification rather than a documented coder note, unlike the federated-optimizer / Adam-no-WD / policy-gradient recodings on other papers.
Q-formers output soft prompts for a frozen LLM.
